title.zh: 卷二十五 卓魯魏劉列傳
title.en: Volume 25: Biography of Zhuo, Lu, Wei, Liu
title.raw: 卷二十五 卓魯魏劉列傳 Volume 25: Biography of Zhuo, Lu, Wei, Liu

sentences.zh: 卓茂字子康，南陽宛人也。

translations.literal: Zhuo Mao, courtesy name Zikang, was a man of Wan in Nanyang.
translations.idiomatic: Zhuo Mao, whose courtesy name was Zikang, came from Wan in Nanyang.

sentences.zh: 父祖皆至郡守。

translations.literal: His father and grandfather both rose to the office of grand administrator of a commandery.
translations.idiomatic: His father and grandfather had both reached the rank of grand administrator.

sentences.zh: 茂，元帝時學於長安，事博士江生，習《詩》、《禮》及歷算。

translations.literal: Mao, during the time of Emperor Yuan, studied in Chang'an, served Erudite Jiang, and studied the Odes, the Rites, and calendrical reckoning.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Yuan, Mao studied in Chang'an under Erudite Jiang, mastering the Odes and Rites as well as astronomy and mathematics.

sentences.zh: 究極師法，稱為通儒。

translations.literal: He exhausted his teachers' methods and was called a thoroughly learned Confucian.
translations.idiomatic: He mastered his teachers' tradition completely and was honored as a scholar of wide learning.

sentences.zh: 性寬仁恭愛。

translations.literal: By nature he was tolerant, humane, respectful, and loving.
translations.idiomatic: He was by nature generous, humane, deferential, and affectionate.

sentences.zh: 鄉黨故舊，雖行能與茂不同，而皆愛慕欣欣焉。

translations.literal: Fellow villagers and old acquaintances, although their conduct and abilities differed from Mao's, all loved and admired him happily.
translations.idiomatic: Neighbors and old friends whose talents and conduct did not match his still cherished him warmly.

sentences.zh: 初辟丞相府史，事孔光，光稱為長者。

translations.literal: At first he was summoned as a clerk in the Chancellor's office; he served Kong Guang, who praised him as a man of mature virtue.
translations.idiomatic: He began his career as a clerk in the Chancellor's office under Kong Guang, who singled him out as a gentleman of substance.

sentences.zh: 時嘗出行，有人認其馬。

translations.literal: Once when he went out on business, someone claimed his horse.
translations.idiomatic: On one occasion when he was traveling, a stranger insisted the horse was his.

sentences.zh: 茂問曰：『子亡馬幾何時？

translations.literal: Mao asked, "How long has your horse been missing?
translations.idiomatic: Mao asked him, "How long ago did you lose your horse?

sentences.zh: 』對曰：『月餘日矣。

translations.literal: " He replied, "More than a month.
translations.idiomatic: " The man answered, "Over a month ago.

sentences.zh: 』茂有馬數年，心知其謬，嘿解與之，挽車而去，顧曰：『若非公馬，幸至丞相府歸我。

translations.literal: " Mao had owned the horse for several years and knew the claim was wrong; silently he untied it and handed it over, pulled his cart away, looked back, and said, "If it is not your horse, please bring it to the Chancellor's office and return it to me.
translations.idiomatic: " Mao had ridden that horse for years and knew the other man was mistaken; without a word he gave up the animal, hitched his cart himself, and called over his shoulder that if the horse was not truly the man's, he should return it to him at the Chancellor's compound.

sentences.zh: 』他日，馬主別得亡者，乃諸府送馬，叩頭謝之。

translations.literal: " Another day the horse's owner recovered his lost horse elsewhere; then he went to the office to return the horse and kowtowed in apology.
translations.idiomatic: " Some days later the claimant found his actual lost mount and brought Mao's horse back to the office, kowtowing in apology.

sentences.zh: 茂性不好爭如此。

translations.literal: Such was Mao's nature—not fond of quarreling.
translations.idiomatic: This was typical of Mao: he had little stomach for contention.

sentences.zh: 後以儒術舉為侍郎，給事黃門，遷密令。

translations.literal: Later he was recommended on account of Confucian learning and appointed Gentleman-in-Attendance of the Household, served in the Yellow Gates, and was promoted to magistrate of Mi.
translations.idiomatic: He was later recommended for his scholarship and rose to Gentleman-in-Attendance of the Household, served at the Yellow Gates, and was transferred to magistrate of Mi county.

sentences.zh: 勞心諄諄，視人如子，舉善而教，口無惡言，吏人親愛而不忍欺之。

translations.literal: He toiled earnestly and tirelessly, regarded people as his children, lifted up the good and instructed them, spoke no harsh words, and clerks and commoners loved him and could not bear to deceive him.
translations.idiomatic: He poured himself into the office, treating the people like his own children: he praised virtue and taught by example, never spoke cruelly, and his officials and subjects grew so fond of him that none could bring themselves to betray his trust.

sentences.zh: 人嘗有言部享長受其米肉遺者，茂辟左右問之曰：『亭長為從汝求乎？

translations.literal: Someone once reported that the pavilion chief had accepted grain and meat given him as gifts; Mao dismissed those beside him and asked the man, "Did the pavilion chief demand these from you?
translations.idiomatic: Once a complainant accused the local pavilion chief of accepting gifts of grain and meat; Mao sent his attendants away and asked whether the chief had demanded those things.

sentences.zh: 為汝有事囑之而受乎？

translations.literal: Did he accept because you had business and entrusted it to him?
translations.idiomatic: Had the chief taken them because you asked a favor of him?

sentences.zh: 將平居自以恩意遺之乎？

translations.literal: Or did you of your own accord in ordinary times give them out of kindly feeling?
translations.idiomatic: Or had you simply offered them on your own out of goodwill?

sentences.zh: 』人曰：『往遺之耳。

translations.literal: " The man said, "I went and gave them to him.
translations.idiomatic: " The man replied, "I brought them of my own accord.

sentences.zh: 』茂曰：『遺之而受，何故言邪？

translations.literal: " Mao said, "You gave them and he accepted—why then speak of it?
translations.idiomatic: " Mao said, "You offered a gift and he accepted—why complain?

sentences.zh: 』人曰：『竊聞賢明之君，使人不畏吏，吏不取人。

translations.literal: " The man said, "I have heard that under a worthy and enlightened ruler the people need not fear officials, and officials do not take from the people.
translations.idiomatic: " The man answered, "I have always heard that a wise ruler keeps subjects from fearing their clerks and clerks from exploiting the people.

sentences.zh: 今我畏吏，是以遺之，吏既卒受，故來言耳。

translations.literal: " Now I fear the official; therefore I gave them to him. Since the official has already accepted them in the end, therefore I have come to speak of it.
translations.idiomatic: " I am afraid of that clerk, so I tried to placate him with gifts; now that he has taken them, I felt I had to report it.

sentences.zh: 』茂曰：『汝為敝人矣。

translations.literal: " Mao said, "You are a shallow person.
translations.idiomatic: " Mao replied, "Your judgment is poor indeed.

sentences.zh: 凡人所以貴於禽獸者，以有仁愛，知相敬事也。

translations.literal: The reason human beings are esteemed above birds and beasts is that they possess humane love and know how mutually to respect one another in conduct.
translations.idiomatic: What elevates us above the beasts is benevolence and the sense to treat one another with respect.

sentences.zh: 今鄰裏長老尚致饋遺，此乃人道所以相親，況吏與民乎？

translations.literal: Now neighborhood elders still send gifts—this is how human relations draw close to one another—how much more so between officials and common people?
translations.idiomatic: Even elders in the neighborhood exchange gifts to draw closer; should not an official and the people do the same?

sentences.zh: 吏顧不當乘威力強請求耳。

translations.literal: An official only ought not ride his authority to force demands.
translations.idiomatic: What an official must never do is abuse his power to extort favors.

sentences.zh: 凡人之生，群居雜處，故有經紀禮義以相交接。

translations.literal: All human life dwells in mixed communities; therefore there are norms and ritual propriety by which to associate with one another.
translations.idiomatic: We live among others in society, which is why ritual and moral duty govern how we treat one another.

sentences.zh: 汝濁不欲修之，寧能高飛遠走，不在人間邪？

translations.literal: You are base and do not wish to cultivate these—can you soar high and flee far and not be among human beings?
translations.idiomatic: You refuse to live up to that standard—do you imagine you can fly away and leave the human world behind?

sentences.zh: 亭長素善吏，歲時遺之，禮也。

translations.literal: The pavilion chief has long been on good terms with the official; at year's turning he sends gifts—this is ritual.
translations.idiomatic: Your chief has always been friendly with that clerk; seasonal gifts between them are simply courtesy.

sentences.zh: 』人曰：『茍如此，律何故禁之？

translations.literal: " The man said, "If it is so, why does the statute forbid it?
translations.idiomatic: " The man objected, "If that is proper, why does the law ban it?

sentences.zh: 』茂笑曰：『律設大法，禮順人情。

translations.literal: " Mao smiled and said, "Statutes set forth great principles; ritual yields to human feeling.
translations.idiomatic: " Mao smiled and answered, "Law lays down broad rules; ritual accommodates human sentiment.

sentences.zh: 今我以禮教汝，汝必無怨惡；

translations.literal: Now if I instruct you with ritual, you surely will have no resentment or hatred;
translations.idiomatic: If I guide you with ritual, you will harbor no bitterness;

sentences.zh: 以律治汝，何所措其手足乎？

translations.literal: if I govern you with statutes, where will you put hands and feet?
translations.idiomatic: if I hammer you with the code, you will not know where to turn.

sentences.zh: 一門之內，小者可論，大者可殺也。

translations.literal: Within one gate, small matters may be debated; great matters may warrant execution.
translations.idiomatic: Under my roof petty faults may be argued out; grave ones can cost a life.

sentences.zh: 且歸念之！

translations.literal: Go home and reflect on it!
translations.idiomatic: Go home and think it through.

sentences.zh: 』於是人納其訓，吏懷其恩。

translations.literal: " Thereupon the man accepted his instruction, and the official cherished his kindness.
translations.idiomatic: The complainant took his lesson to heart, and the clerk felt gratitude for his consideration.

sentences.zh: 初，茂到縣，有所廢置，吏人笑之，鄰城聞者皆蚩其不能。

translations.literal: At first, when Mao arrived in the county he abolished and established some things; clerks and people laughed at him, and those in neighboring counties who heard of it all sneered at his inability.
translations.idiomatic: When Mao first took office he reorganized several offices; locals mocked him, and neighboring counties scoffed at his inexperience.

sentences.zh: 河南郡為置守令，茂不能嫌，理事自若。

translations.literal: Henan commandery appointed an acting magistrate for him; Mao could not resent it and conducted affairs as before.
translations.idiomatic: Henan commandery even assigned an acting magistrate over him; Mao swallowed his pride and kept administering as calmly as ever.

sentences.zh: 數年，教化大行，道不拾遺。

translations.literal: After several years, moral transformation was broadly practiced and nothing lost on the road went unpicked up.
translations.idiomatic: Within a few years his moral sway reached everywhere; people left lost goods untouched in the streets.

sentences.zh: 平帝時，天下大蝗，河南二十餘縣皆被其災，獨不入密縣界。

translations.literal: During Emperor Ping's reign there were great locusts in the realm; more than twenty counties in Henan all suffered the calamity, but alone they did not enter Mi county's borders.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Ping a devastating locust plague swept the empire; Henan lost over twenty counties to it, yet the swarm never crossed into Mi.

sentences.zh: 督郵言之，太守不信，自出案行，見乃服焉。

translations.literal: The inspector reported it; the grand administrator did not believe it and went out himself to inspect on patrol; when he saw it he then submitted.
translations.idiomatic: The circuit inspector reported this; the grand administrator doubted the story until he toured the region himself and then conceded Mao's achievement.

sentences.zh: 是時，王莽秉政，置大司農六部丞，勸課農桑。

translations.literal: At this time Wang Mang held power; he established six assistant ministers under the Minister of Agriculture and urged farming and sericulture.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Mang was then in charge; he added six assistant ministers under the Minister of Agriculture to promote farming and silk production.

sentences.zh: 遷茂為京部丞，密人老少皆涕泣隨送。

translations.literal: Mao was promoted to assistant for the capital region; old and young of Mi wept and followed to send him off.
translations.idiomatic: Mao was transferred to assistant magistrate for the capital circuit; the whole county, young and old, wept as they escorted him away.

sentences.zh: 及莽居攝，以病免歸郡，常為門下掾祭酒，不肯作職吏。

translations.literal: When Mang acted as regent, Mao took illness and retired to his commandery, regularly serving as libationer among retainers and refusing to serve as a duty clerk.
translations.idiomatic: When Wang Mang ruled as regent, Mao pleaded illness and returned home, acting only as a scholar-adviser among local retainers and refusing substantive office.

sentences.zh: 更始立，以茂為侍中祭酒，從至長安，知更始政亂，以年老乞骸骨歸。

translations.literal: When Gengshi was established, Mao was appointed Libationer among Palace Attendants and followed to Chang'an; knowing Gengshi's government was chaotic, he asked for his bones on account of old age and returned.
translations.idiomatic: After Emperor Gengshi took the throne Mao became Libationer among Palace Attendants and accompanied the court to Chang'an; seeing how chaotic the regime was, he cited age and retired home.

sentences.zh: 時，光武初即位，先訪求茂，茂詣河陽謁見。

translations.literal: At this time, when Guangwu had first ascended the throne, he first sought out Mao; Mao went to Heyang for audience.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu had scarcely taken the throne when he sent for Zhuo Mao; Mao traveled to Heyang for his audience.

sentences.zh: 乃下詔曰『前密令卓茂，束身自修，執節淳固，誠能為人所不能為。

translations.literal: Thereupon an edict was issued saying, "The former magistrate of Mi, Zhuo Mao, restrained himself and cultivated himself, held to integrity purely and firmly—truly able to do what others cannot do.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor then proclaimed: "The former magistrate of Mi, Zhuo Mao, disciplined himself, lived plainly, and held fast to principle—he did what ordinary men cannot.

sentences.zh: 夫名冠天下，當受天下重賞，故武王誅紂，封比幹之墓，表商容之閭。

translations.literal: One whose fame crowns the realm ought to receive the realm's weighty reward; therefore King Wu punished Zhou and sealed Bi Gan's tomb and marked Shang Rong's lane.
translations.idiomatic: A reputation that towers over the empire deserves the empire's richest honors—King Wu slew Zhou yet honored Bi Gan's grave and marked Shang Rong's gate.

sentences.zh: 今以茂為太傅，封褒德侯，食邑二千戶，賜幾杖、車馬，衣一襲，絮五百斤。

translations.literal: Now appoint Mao as Grand Tutor, enfeoff him as Marquis Who Extols Virtue with two thousand household fief, grant him a bench and staff, carriage and horses, one suit of clothes, and five hundred jin of silk wadding.
translations.idiomatic: We therefore name Mao Grand Tutor and Marquis Who Extols Virtue with a fief of two thousand households, and present him with an honor seat and staff, a carriage team, a full set of robes, and five hundred jin of padded silk.

sentences.zh: 』復以茂長子戎為太中大夫，次子崇為中郎，給事黃門。

translations.literal: " Moreover appoint Mao's eldest son Rong as Grand Counselor of the Palace, second son Chong as Gentleman of the Household, to serve in the Yellow Gates.
translations.idiomatic: " Mao's eldest son Rong is named Grand Counselor of the Palace and his second son Chong Gentleman of the Household in attendance at the Yellow Gates.

sentences.zh: 建武四年，薨，賜棺槨冢地，車駕素服親臨送葬。

translations.literal: In the fourth year of Jianwu he died; a coffin, outer shell, and burial ground were granted; the imperial carriage in plain dress personally attended the funeral.
translations.idiomatic: He died in Jianwu 4; the court supplied coffin, burial vault, and tomb ground, and the emperor himself, in mourning white, attended the burial.

sentences.zh: 子崇嗣，徙封汎鄉侯，官至大司農。

translations.literal: His son Chong succeeded; the enfeoffment was moved to Marquis of Fan township; his office reached Minister of Agriculture.
translations.idiomatic: His son Chong inherited the title, which was relocated to Fan township; Chong rose to Minister of Agriculture.

sentences.zh: 崇卒，子棽嗣。

translations.literal: When Chong died, his son Chen succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: Chong died and was succeeded by his son Chen.

sentences.zh: 棽卒，子訢嗣。

translations.literal: When Chen died, his son Xin succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: Chen died and Xin inherited the marquisate.

sentences.zh: 訢卒，子隆嗣。

translations.literal: When Xin died, his son Long succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: Xin was succeeded by his son Long.

sentences.zh: 永元十五年，隆卒，無子，國除。

translations.literal: In the fifteenth year of Yongyuan, Long died without a son; the state was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongyuan 15 Long died heirless and the marquisate was extinguished.

sentences.zh: 論曰：建武之初，雄豪方擾，虓呼者連響，嬰城者相望，斯固倥傯不暇給之日。

translations.literal: The historian's judgment says: At the beginning of Jianwu, heroic strongmen were everywhere in turmoil; those who roared answered one another in succession; those who defended walled cities faced one another across the landscape—this was truly a day of pressing urgency when nothing could be spared.
translations.idiomatic: The appraisal: In the early Jianwu years warlords churned the realm—battle cries echoed from column to column and rival garrisons faced off across the landscape; the empire barely kept pace with its crises.

sentences.zh: 卓茂斷斷小宰，無它庸能，時已七十餘矣，而首加聘命，優辭重禮，其與周、燕之君表閭立館何異哉？

translations.literal: Zhuo Mao was an upright petty magistrate without other mediocre abilities; already he was over seventy, yet first appointment was conferred on him with gracious words and heavy ritual—how does this differ from the rulers of Zhou and Yan marking lanes and establishing lodges?
translations.idiomatic: Zhuo Mao was only an honest county officer without dazzling talent—past seventy—yet Guangwu singled him out first with lavish honors; how was that any less than ancient kings honoring virtuous commoners with inscribed gates and endowed halls?

sentences.zh: 於是蘊憤歸道之賓，越關阻，捐宗族，以排金門者眾矣。

translations.literal: Thereupon guests harboring resentment who returned to the Way, crossed barriers and passes, cast aside clan and kin to crowd toward the golden gates—were many.
translations.idiomatic: Scholars nursing grievances yet cleaving to principle crossed frontier barriers and left family estates behind to throng the palace gates.

sentences.zh: 夫厚性寬中近於仁，犯而不校鄰於恕，率斯道也，怨悔曷其至乎！

translations.literal: Now a generous nature and broad center approach benevolence; being offended yet not retaliating borders on forgiveness—whoever follows this Way, how could resentment and regret reach him!
translations.idiomatic: A generous temper and calm heart skirt benevolence; to endure insult without answering approaches true forgiveness—live like that, and what room remains for bitterness?

sentences.zh: 魯恭字仲康，扶風平陵人也。

translations.literal: Lu Gong, courtesy name Zhongkang, was a man of Pingling in Fufeng.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Gong, styled Zhongkang, was a native of Pingling in Fufeng.

sentences.zh: 其先出於魯頃公，為楚所滅，遷於下邑，因氏焉。

translations.literal: His ancestors came from Duke Qing of Lu; when Lu was destroyed by Chu they moved to Xia yi and took their surname there.
translations.idiomatic: The family traced its line to Duke Qing of Lu; after Chu conquered Lu they settled in Xia yi and adopted Lu as their clan name.

sentences.zh: 世吏二千石，哀、平間，自魯而徙。

translations.literal: For generations they served as officials at two thousand shi rank; between Emperors Ai and Ping they moved from Lu.
translations.idiomatic: For generations they held salary ranks of two thousand shi; during the Ai and Ping reigns they relocated from Lu.

sentences.zh: 祖父匡，王莽時，為羲和，有權數，號曰『智囊』。

translations.literal: His grandfather Kuang, during Wang Mang's time, was Director of Harmonizing the Seasons; he had stratagems and was called 'the wisdom satchel.'
translations.idiomatic: His grandfather Kuang served Wang Mang as Director of Harmonizing the Seasons—clever with policy and nicknamed 'the wisdom satchel.'

sentences.zh: 父某。

translations.literal: His father's personal name is given only as Mou.
translations.idiomatic: His father's name was not recorded beyond the placeholder Mou.

sentences.zh: 建武初，為武陵太守，卒官。

translations.literal: At the beginning of Jianwu he was grand administrator of Wuling and died in office.
translations.idiomatic: Early in Jianwu he became grand administrator of Wuling and died in that post.

sentences.zh: 時恭年十二，弟丕七歲，晝夜號踴不絕聲，郡中賻贈無所受，乃歸服喪，禮過成人，鄉裏奇之。

translations.literal: At that time Gong was twelve and his younger brother Pi seven; day and night they wailed and leaped without stopping their voices; they accepted none of the commandery's condolence gifts; then they returned to observe mourning; their ritual exceeded adults; the village marveled at them.
translations.idiomatic: Gong was twelve and Pi seven when their father died; they keened without pause and refused the commandery's funeral contributions, then went home to mourn with rites beyond what boys their age were expected to observe—the neighbors were astonished.

sentences.zh: 十五，與母及丕俱居太學，習《魯詩》，閉戶講誦，絕人間事，兄弟俱為諸儒所稱，學士爭歸之。

translations.literal: At fifteen he together with his mother and Pi all dwelt in the Imperial Academy, studying the Lu version of the Odes; they closed doors and lectured and recited, cutting off worldly affairs; both brothers were praised by the various Confucians; students contended to attach to them.
translations.idiomatic: At fifteen he moved into the Imperial Academy with his mother and Pi to study the Lu Odes behind closed doors, ignoring the world; both brothers won praise from the faculty and students flocked to study under them.

sentences.zh: 太尉趙憙慕其誌，每歲時遣子問以酒糧，皆辭不受。

translations.literal: Grand Commandant Zhao Xi admired his intent; every season he sent his son with gifts of wine and grain; all were declined and not accepted.
translations.idiomatic: Grand Commandant Zhao Xi admired his integrity and sent seasonal gifts of wine and grain through his son; Gong courteously refused every time.

sentences.zh: 恭憐丕小，欲先就其名，托疾不仕。

translations.literal: Gong pitied Pi's youth and wished first to establish Pi's reputation; he pleaded illness and did not take office.
translations.idiomatic: Because Pi was still young, Gong held back his own career so his brother might shine first, claiming illness to avoid appointment.

sentences.zh: 郡數以禮請，謝不肯應，母強遣之，恭不得已而西，因留新豐教授。

translations.literal: The commandery repeatedly summoned him with ritual propriety; he declined and would not respond; his mother forced him to go; Gong had no choice but to go west and therefore stayed at Xinfeng to teach.
translations.idiomatic: The commandery invited him repeatedly with due ceremony; he declined until his mother insisted; he finally traveled west and settled in Xinfeng as a teacher.

sentences.zh: 建初初，丕舉方正，恭始為郡吏。

translations.literal: At the beginning of Jianchu, Pi was recommended as upright and honest; Gong then became a commandery clerk.
translations.idiomatic: Early in the Jianchu era Pi earned recommendation as 'upright and honest,' after which Gong accepted a post as commandery clerk.

sentences.zh: 太傅趙憙聞而辟之。

translations.literal: Grand Tutor Zhao Xi heard of it and summoned him.
translations.idiomatic: Grand Tutor Zhao Xi heard of him and brought him onto his staff.

sentences.zh: 肅宗集諸儒於白虎觀，恭特以經明得召，與其議。

translations.literal: Emperor Suzong assembled the Confucians at White Tiger Hall; Gong was specially summoned because of his clarity on the classics and participated in their discussions.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Zhang convened scholars at White Tiger Hall, Gong was invited on strength of his classical expertise and joined the debates.

sentences.zh: 憙復舉恭直言，特詔公車，拜中牟令。

translations.literal: Xi again recommended Gong for blunt counsel; he was specially summoned by edict to the Waiting Coaches office and appointed magistrate of Zhongmou.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Xi next recommended him for forthright counsel; an edict routed him through the Waiting Coaches office and named him magistrate of Zhongmou.

sentences.zh: 恭專以德化為理，不任刑罰，訟人許伯等爭田，累守令不能決，恭為平理曲直，皆退而自責，輟耕相讓。

translations.literal: Gong devoted himself to governing through moral transformation and did not rely on punishments; litigants such as Xu Bo disputed fields; successive grand administrators and magistrates could not decide; Gong for them settled right and wrong; all withdrew and blamed themselves, stopped plowing and yielded to one another.
translations.idiomatic: Gong ruled by moral suasion rather than the lash. When Xu Bo and others sued over farmland—cases prior magistrates could not untangle—Gong weighed the rights of each party so fairly that both sides blamed themselves, left off farming, and ceded ground to each other.

sentences.zh: 亭長從人借牛而不肯還之，牛主訟於恭。

translations.literal: A pavilion chief borrowed someone's ox and refused to return it; the ox's owner sued Gong.
translations.idiomatic: A village pavilion chief borrowed an ox and would not give it back; the owner appealed to Gong.

sentences.zh: 恭召亭長，敕令歸牛者再三，猶不從。

translations.literal: Gong summoned the pavilion chief and ordered him to return the ox repeatedly again and again; still he did not obey.
translations.idiomatic: Gong called the chief in and repeatedly ordered him to return the ox, yet the man defied him.

sentences.zh: 恭嘆曰：『是教化不行也。

translations.literal: Gong sighed and said, "This means moral transformation is not practiced.
translations.idiomatic: Gong sighed, "So much for moral sway in this county.

sentences.zh: 』欲解印綬去。

translations.literal: " He wished to untie his seal-cord and leave.
translations.idiomatic: " He prepared to resign his seal and walk away.

sentences.zh: 掾史涕泣共留之，亭長乃慚悔，還牛，詣獄受罪，薛貰不問。

translations.literal: Clerks wept together and detained him; the pavilion chief then felt shame and regret, returned the ox, went to prison to receive punishment; Gong pardoned him and pressed the matter no further.
translations.idiomatic: His staff wept and urged him to stay; the pavilion chief, ashamed, returned the ox and surrendered to jail, but Gong forgave him outright.

sentences.zh: 於是吏人信服。

translations.literal: Thereupon clerks and people trusted and submitted.
translations.idiomatic: Officials and commoners alike came to trust him completely.

sentences.zh: 建初七年，郡國螟傷稼，犬牙緣界，不入中牟。

translations.literal: In the seventh year of Jianchu, caterpillars in the commanderies and kingdoms harmed the crops; along boundaries like dogs' teeth they did not enter Zhongmou.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianchu 7 crop-eating caterpillars ravaged neighboring jurisdictions along zigzag borders yet spared Zhongmou entirely.

sentences.zh: 河南尹袁安聞之，疑其不實，使仁恕掾肥親往廉之。

translations.literal: Governor of Henan Yuan An heard of it and doubted its truth; he sent Assistant for Benevolence Fei Qin to go investigate it.
translations.idiomatic: Henan's governor Yuan An doubted the reports and dispatched his investigator Fei Qin to verify them.

sentences.zh: 恭隨行阡陌，俱坐桑下，有雉過，止其傍。

translations.literal: Gong accompanied him walking field paths; together they sat under mulberries; a pheasant passed and stopped beside them.
translations.idiomatic: Gong walked the fields with him and they rested beneath a mulberry; a pheasant fluttered down beside them.

sentences.zh: 傍有童兒，親曰：『兒何不捕之？

translations.literal: Beside them was a boy; Qin said, "Boy, why do you not catch it?
translations.idiomatic: A child stood nearby; Fei asked why he did not snatch the bird.

sentences.zh: 』兒言：『雉方將雛。

translations.literal: " The boy said, "The pheasant is about to tend chicks.
translations.idiomatic: " The boy said it was brooding chicks.

sentences.zh: 』親瞿然而起，與恭訣曰：『所以來者，欲察君之政跡耳。

translations.literal: " Qin started up in alarm and took leave of Gong, saying, "The reason I came was to inspect your administrative traces.
translations.idiomatic: " Fei rose in astonishment and told Gong he had seen enough of his governance.

sentences.zh: 今蟲不犯境，此一異也；

translations.literal: Now insects do not violate the borders—this is one marvel;
translations.idiomatic: First marvel: pests halt at your border;

sentences.zh: 化及鳥獸，此二異也；

translations.literal: transformation reaches birds and beasts—this is a second marvel;
translations.idiomatic: second: your virtue touches even birds and beasts;

sentences.zh: 豎子有仁心，此三異也。

translations.literal: the stripling has a humane heart—this is a third marvel.
translations.idiomatic: third: even a child here shows compassion.

sentences.zh: 久留，徒擾賢者耳。

translations.literal: To remain long would merely disturb the worthy.
translations.idiomatic: To stay longer would only burden a worthy official.

sentences.zh: 』還府，具以狀白安。

translations.literal: " He returned to the office and reported the whole situation to An in detail.
translations.idiomatic: He rode back to the governor's yamen and reported everything to Yuan An.

sentences.zh: 是歲，嘉禾生恭便坐廷中，安因上書言狀，帝異之。

translations.literal: That year, auspicious grain grew in Gong's casual sitting hall in the courtyard; An therefore submitted a memorial stating the situation; the emperor marveled at it.
translations.idiomatic: That same year stalks of auspicious grain sprouted outside Gong's casual office; Yuan memorialized the throne and the emperor took note.

sentences.zh: 會詔百官舉賢良方正，恭薦中牟名士王方，帝即征方詣公車，禮之與公卿所舉同，方致位侍中。

translations.literal: It happened that an edict ordered the hundred officials to recommend worthy and upright men; Gong recommended Zhongmou's eminent scholar Wang Fang; the emperor immediately summoned Fang to the Waiting Coaches office and treated him the same as those recommended by dukes and ministers; Fang reached the office of Palace Attendant.
translations.idiomatic: When the court called for worthy candidates, Gong nominated the local scholar Wang Fang; the emperor summoned Fang with the same honors accorded nominees of high ministers, and Fang rose to Palace Attendant.

sentences.zh: 恭在事三年，州舉尤異，會遭母喪去官，吏人思之。

translations.literal: Gong was in office three years; the province recommended him as outstandingly unusual; it happened he encountered mother's mourning and left office; clerks and people missed him.
translations.idiomatic: After three exemplary years the province flagged him for special distinction, but mourning for his mother forced him to resign; the county longed for his return.

sentences.zh: 後拜侍禦史。

translations.literal: Later he was appointed Palace Assistant Clerk.
translations.idiomatic: He was later promoted to Palace Assistant Clerk.

sentences.zh: 和帝初立，議遣車騎將軍竇憲與征西將軍耿秉擊匈奴，恭上疏諫曰：

translations.literal: When Emperor He had first been established, they debated sending General of Chariots and Cavalry Dou Xian and General Who Conquers the West Geng Bing to strike the Xiongnu; Gong submitted a memorial remonstrating, saying:
translations.idiomatic: Early in Emperor He's reign the court debated dispatching Dou Xian and Geng Bing against the Xiongnu; Gong filed a memorial of opposition:

sentences.zh: 陛下親勞聖思，日昊不食，憂在軍役，誠欲以安定北垂，為人除患，定萬世之計也。

translations.literal: Your Majesty personally labors sagely thought, the sun sets and you do not eat; your worry lies in military service; truly you desire thereby to pacify the northern marches, remove harm for the people, and settle planning for ten thousand generations.
translations.idiomatic: You exhaust yourself—fasting past sundown—because military burdens weigh on your mind; you genuinely mean to secure the northern frontier, spare the people calamity, and fix policy for generations to come.

sentences.zh: 臣伏獨思之，未見其便。

translations.literal: Your servant respectfully ponders alone and does not see its convenience.
translations.idiomatic: Yet weighing the matter alone, I see no clear advantage in this campaign.

sentences.zh: 社稷之計，萬人之命，在於一舉。

translations.literal: The planning for altars of soil and grain, the lives of ten thousand men—all hinge on this single undertaking.
translations.idiomatic: The fate of the state and tens of thousands of lives hang on this one decision.

sentences.zh: 數年以來，秋稼不熟，人食不足，倉庫空虛，國無畜積。

translations.literal: For several years now autumn crops have not ripened; people's food is insufficient; granaries stand empty; the state has no stored surplus.
translations.idiomatic: Year after year the autumn harvest has failed, bellies go unfed, granaries are bare, and the treasury holds no reserve.

sentences.zh: 會新遭大憂，人懷恐懼。

translations.literal: Moreover we have newly suffered great mourning; people harbor fear.
translations.idiomatic: The court has just endured deep mourning, and the people are still afraid.

sentences.zh: 陛下躬大聖之德，履至孝之行，盡諒陰三年，聽於冢宰。

translations.literal: Your Majesty embodies the virtue of the greatest sage and fulfills the utmost filial conduct; you have completed three full years in the mourning hut and entrusted affairs to the Senior Steward.
translations.idiomatic: You embody supreme sagely virtue and perfect filial piety—you observed three years of mourning in seclusion and left governance to the chief minister.

sentences.zh: 百姓闕然，三時不聞警蹕之音，莫不懷思皇皇，若有求而不得。

translations.literal: The hundred clans stand bereft; through three seasons they have not heard the sounds of clearance drums and riding patrols; none does not long anxiously, as though seeking yet unable to obtain.
translations.idiomatic: The common folk feel bereft: three seasons without hearing your traveling court; everyone aches for a glimpse of you as if hungering for something just out of reach.

sentences.zh: 今乃以盛春之月，興發軍役，擾動天下，以事戎夷，誠非所以垂恩中國，改元正時，由內及外也。

translations.literal: Now in the full vigor of spring you raise military levies and shake the realm to serve the barbarians—this truly is not the means by which to extend grace within the Middle Kingdom, rectify the era's calendar, and proceed from inner to outer.
translations.idiomatic: Yet now, at the height of spring, you would mobilize armies and convulse the empire against frontier tribes—hardly the way to show kindness to the heartland, mark a new reign, and order affairs from the center outward.

sentences.zh: 萬民者，天之所生。

translations.literal: The myriad people are those whom Heaven engenders.
translations.idiomatic: The myriad commoners are Heaven's own creation.

sentences.zh: 天愛其所生，猶父母愛其子。

translations.literal: Heaven loves what it engenders, just as parents love their children.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven cherishes its creatures as parents cherish their children.

sentences.zh: 一物有不得其所者，則天氣為之桀錯，況於人乎？

translations.literal: If even one creature fails to obtain its proper place, then Heaven's qi is thereby thrown awry—how much more so with human beings?
translations.idiomatic: When a single creature is out of place, cosmic harmony falters—what then of mankind?

sentences.zh: 故愛人者必有天報。

translations.literal: Therefore those who love others surely receive Heaven's recompense.
translations.idiomatic: Those who love their fellows are answered by Heaven.

sentences.zh: 昔太王重人命而去邠，故獲上天之祐。

translations.literal: Formerly Grand King Danfu prized human lives and departed Bin; therefore he obtained Heaven's blessing.
translations.idiomatic: The ancient Grand King valued human life above territory when he left Bin behind, and Heaven favored him for it.

sentences.zh: 夫戎狄者，四方之異氣也。

translations.literal: Now the Rong and Di are alien vapors from the four quarters.
translations.idiomatic: The northern tribes are alien to the civilized climate of the central plains.

sentences.zh: 蹲夷踞肆，與鳥獸無別。

translations.literal: They squat like barbarians and sprawl at ease, no different from birds and beasts.
translations.idiomatic: They squat and sprawl like animals—barely distinguishable from the beasts.

sentences.zh: 若雜居中國，則錯亂天氣，汙辱善人，是以聖王之制，羈縻不絕而已。

translations.literal: If they mix among the Middle Kingdom they disorder Heaven's qi and defile good people; therefore in the regulations of sage kings one merely keeps them loosely tethered without severing ties.
translations.idiomatic: Letting them settle among us would corrupt our moral climate and stain the virtuous; sage kings therefore held them at arm's length with loose reins and nothing more.

sentences.zh: 今邊境無事，宜當修仁行義，尚於無為，令家給人足，安業樂產。

translations.literal: Now the borders are quiet; it is fitting to cultivate benevolence and practice righteousness, esteem non-action, and cause households to have surplus and people enough, secure in occupation and content with produce.
translations.idiomatic: The frontier is calm; this is the time to promote benevolence and righteousness, favor quiet governance, and let every family prosper in peace.

sentences.zh: 夫人道乂於下，則陰陽和於上，祥風時雨，覆被遠方，夷狄重譯而至矣。

translations.literal: When human conduct is ordered below, then Yin and Yang harmonize above; auspicious winds and timely rains spread to distant regions, and barbarians come through repeated interpreters.
translations.idiomatic: Order the human realm below and Heaven responds above—with timely winds and rains reaching the farthest marches until foreign peoples arrive through chains of interpreters.

sentences.zh: 《易》曰：『有孕盈缶，終來有它吉。

translations.literal: The Changes says, "There is a pregnancy filling the jar; in the end there comes another auspiciousness."
translations.idiomatic: The Book of Changes reads: "There is something conceived that fills the jar to the brim—ultimately another blessing will come."

sentences.zh: 』言甘雨滿我之缶，誠來有我而吉已。

translations.literal: " It means sweet rain fills my jar; truly what comes includes me and is auspicious.
translations.idiomatic: " It speaks of sweet rain brimming in our vessels—Heaven's favor truly reaches us with good fortune.

sentences.zh: 夫以德勝人者昌，以力勝人者亡。

translations.literal: Now those who overcome others by virtue flourish; those who overcome others by force perish.
translations.idiomatic: Empires built on virtue endure; those built on brute force collapse.

sentences.zh: 今匈奴為鮮卑所殺，遠臧於史侯河西，去塞數千裏，而欲乘其虛耗，利其微弱，是非義之所出也。

translations.literal: Now the Xiongnu have been struck down by the Xianbei and lie far hidden west of the Shi Marches River, thousands of li from the passes—yet you wish to exploit their exhaustion and profit from their weakness; this is not where righteousness proceeds from.
translations.idiomatic: The Xiongnu have already been shattered by the Xianbei and driven deep west of the Shi Marches—thousands of li beyond the frontier—and to strike them now for easy gain is wholly unjust.

sentences.zh: 前太仆祭肜遠出塞外，卒不見一胡而兵已困矣。

translations.literal: Formerly Grand Coachman Ji Rong went far beyond the passes and in the end saw not a single Hu yet the troops were already exhausted.
translations.idiomatic: Grand Coachman Ji Rong once marched deep beyond the frontier without encountering a single barbarian yet still wore his army to exhaustion.

sentences.zh: 自山之難，不絕如綖，都護陷沒，士卒死者如積，迄今被其辜毒。

translations.literal: Since the calamity at the mountains, things have hung by a thread unbroken; the Protector-General was lost and drowned; dead soldiers piled like heaps; down to the present we suffer their poisonous harm.
translations.idiomatic: Since the disaster in the mountains the western frontier has hung by a thread—the Protector-General fell, corpses stacked like cordwood, and the realm still bleeds from that wound.

sentences.zh: 孤寡哀思之心未弭，仁者念之，以為累息，奈何復欲襲其跡，不顧患難乎？

translations.literal: The hearts of widows, orphans, and those grieving in sorrow are not yet soothed; humane men think on it and sigh with cumulative breath—how can you again wish to tread their tracks and disregard calamity and hardship?
translations.idiomatic: Widows and orphans still mourn; any humane ruler pauses at the memory—how can we repeat that march and ignore the suffering it brought?

sentences.zh: 今始征發，而大司農調度不足，使者在道，分部督趣，上下相迫，民間之急亦已甚矣。

translations.literal: Now having only begun conscriptions, the Minister of Agriculture's allocations are insufficient; envoys are on the roads, dividing sections to supervise urgency; high and low press one another; the people's distress is already extreme.
translations.idiomatic: The levies have barely begun yet the exchequer cannot meet them—imperial messengers harry every circuit, officials squeeze one another, and the countryside is at breaking point.

sentences.zh: 三輔、並、涼少雨，麥根枯焦，牛死日甚，此其不合天心之效也。

translations.literal: The Three Adjuncts, Bing, and Liang lack rain; wheat roots are parched; oxen die daily in increasing measure—this is the effect of not accord with Heaven's heart.
translations.idiomatic: The capital region, Bing, and Liang face drought—wheat withers at the root and cattle die by the day—sure signs that Heaven disapproves.

sentences.zh: 群僚百姓，鹹曰不可，陛下獨奈何以一人之計，棄萬人之命，不恤其言乎？

translations.literal: The host of officials and the hundred clans all say it cannot be done—how alone can Your Majesty, for one man's plan, cast aside ten thousand lives and not pity their words?
translations.idiomatic: Every minister and commoner says no—how can you sacrifice countless lives to one man's strategy and ignore their pleas?

sentences.zh: 上觀天心，下察人誌，足以知事之得失。

translations.literal: Look above to Heaven's heart; examine below the people's intent—sufficient to know gain and loss in the affair.
translations.idiomatic: Read Heaven's intent above and the people's will below—the right course is plain.

sentences.zh: 臣恐中國不為中國，豈徒匈奴而已哉！

translations.literal: Your servant fears the Middle Kingdom may cease to be the Middle Kingdom—is it only the Xiongnu?
translations.idiomatic: I fear we may lose the heartland itself—not merely fail against the Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 惟陛下留聖恩，休罷士卒，以順天心。

translations.literal: May Your Majesty retain sagely grace, rest and dismiss the troops, so as to comply with Heaven's heart.
translations.idiomatic: I beg you to show mercy: stand down the armies and align with Heaven.

sentences.zh: 書奏，不從。

translations.literal: The memorial was submitted; it was not followed.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor received the memorial and rejected it.

sentences.zh: 每政事有益於人，恭輒言其便，無所隱諱。

translations.literal: Whenever government affairs benefited people, Gong always spoke their convenience, concealing nothing.
translations.idiomatic: Whenever a policy could help the people, Gong spoke up plainly and hid nothing.

sentences.zh: 其後拜為《魯詩》博士，由是家法學者日盛。

translations.literal: Later he was appointed Erudite in the Lu Odes; henceforth scholars of his household tradition daily flourished.
translations.idiomatic: He was later named Erudite of the Lu Odes, and disciples of his scholarly line multiplied.

sentences.zh: 遷侍中，數召宴見，問以得失，賞賜恩禮寵異焉。

translations.literal: He was promoted to Palace Attendant; repeatedly he was summoned to feast audiences and questioned on gain and loss; rewards, grace, and ritual favors distinguished him.
translations.idiomatic: Promoted to Palace Attendant, he was often summoned to informal audiences over meals, consulted on policy, and singled out for honors.

sentences.zh: 遷樂安相。

translations.literal: He was promoted to chancellor of Lean.
translations.idiomatic: He went on to serve as chancellor of the kingdom of Lean.

sentences.zh: 是時，東州多盜賊，群輩攻劫，諸郡患之。

translations.literal: At this time in the eastern provinces there were many bandits; gangs attacked and plundered; the various commanderies suffered from them.
translations.idiomatic: The eastern circuits swarmed with bandit gangs that raided commanderies at will.

sentences.zh: 恭到，重購賞，開恩信，其渠帥張漢等率支黨降，恭上以漢補博昌尉，其餘遂自相捕擊，盡破平之，州郡以安。

translations.literal: When Gong arrived he doubled rewards for capture and opened grace and trust; their chief Zhang Han and others led branches and factions to surrender; Gong memorialized to appoint Han as captain of Bochang; the remainder then mutually captured and struck one another; all were utterly broken and pacified; province and commandery were thereby secure.
translations.idiomatic: Gong offered heavy bounties and won the bandits' trust; chiefs such as Zhang Han surrendered with their followers, and Gong recommended Zhang for a county captaincy. The remainder turned on one another until every band was crushed and peace returned.

sentences.zh: 初，和帝末，下令麥秋得案驗薄刑，而州郡好以苛察為政，因此遂盛夏斷獄。

translations.literal: Formerly, near the end of Emperor He's reign, an order went out that at wheat harvest minor punishments might be investigated; but commanderies and provinces liked to govern by harsh scrutiny; therefore they consequently tried prison cases in midsummer.
translations.idiomatic: Late in Emperor He's reign the court allowed review of light sentences after the wheat harvest, yet local officials preferred harsh scrutiny and began holding criminal trials in the heat of summer.

sentences.zh: 恭上疏諫曰：

translations.literal: Gong submitted a memorial remonstrating, saying:
translations.idiomatic: Gong filed a memorial of objection:

sentences.zh: 臣伏見詔書，敬若天時，憂念萬民，為崇和氣，罪非殊死，且勿案驗。

translations.literal: Your servant respectfully has seen the edict book—revering it like seasons of Heaven, worrying over the myriad people, so as to elevate harmonious qi; crimes not capital—temporarily do not investigate them.
translations.idiomatic: Your recent edicts honor Heaven's seasons and care for the people by nurturing harmonious qi—you ordered that noncapital cases not be pursued for the moment.

sentences.zh: 進柔良，退貪殘，奉時令。

translations.literal: Advance the gentle and good; retire the greedy and cruel; uphold seasonal ordinances.
translations.idiomatic: Promote the gentle and worthy, dismiss the cruel and corrupt, and heed the seasonal calendar.

sentences.zh: 所以助仁德，順昊天，致和氣，利黎民者也。

translations.literal: This is the means by which to assist humane virtue, comply with vast Heaven, bring harmonious qi, and benefit the black-haired people.
translations.idiomatic: That is how we bolster humane rule, align with Heaven, summon auspicious qi, and bless the common folk.

sentences.zh: 舊制至立秋乃行薄刑，自永元十五年以來，改用孟夏，而刺史、太守不深惟憂民息事之原，進良退殘之化，因以盛夏征召農人，拘對考驗，連滯無已。

translations.literal: Former regulation was that light punishments were carried out only when Autumn Begins; since the fifteenth year of Yongyuan it has been changed to employ first summer; yet inspectors and grand administrators do not deeply ponder the root of worrying for the people and resting affairs, the transformation of advancing the good and retiring the cruel; therefore in deep summer they summon farmers, detain them face-to-face for examination and verification, dragging on without cease.
translations.idiomatic: Under old rules minor punishments waited until Autumn Begins; since Yongyuan 15 courts have shifted hearings into early summer. Inspectors and magistrates seldom weigh the need to spare farmers during growing season—they drag peasants from the fields for endless interrogations.

sentences.zh: 司隸典司京師，四方是則，而近於春月分行諸部，托言勞來貧人，而無隱惻之實，煩擾郡縣，廉考非急，逮捕一人，罪延十數，上逆時氣，下傷農業。

translations.literal: The Metropolitan Superintendent oversees the capital and is the model for the four quarters; yet near spring months he divides lines among departments, falsely claiming to console poor people, yet without real hidden sympathy; vexing commanderies and counties; investigating examinations not urgent; arresting one person, guilt extends to ten or more; above they oppose seasonal qi; below they harm farming.
translations.idiomatic: The Metropolitan Superintendent should set the standard for the empire, yet each spring his agents fan out claiming to aid the poor while harassing counties—snaring one suspect implicates dozens, defying seasonal harmony and ruining the harvest.

sentences.zh: 案《易》氣月《後》用事。

translations.literal: Investigate the Changes: in the qi month the Hou hexagram line holds office.
translations.idiomatic: The Book of Changes assigns the Hou line to govern the qi month when Yin begins to stir.

sentences.zh: 經曰：『後以施令誥四方。

translations.literal: The classic says, "The queen issues commands and announces them to the four quarters."
translations.idiomatic: The classic states: "The queen issues commands to the four quarters."

sentences.zh: 』言君以夏至之日，施命令止四方行者，所以助微陰也。

translations.literal: " It means the ruler on the day of summer solstice issues commands stopping travelers of the four quarters—this is the means to assist faint Yin.
translations.idiomatic: " At the summer solstice the ruler halts travelers everywhere to nurture the first faint Yin force.

sentences.zh: 行者尚止之，況於逮召考掠，奪其時哉！

translations.literal: Travelers are still stopped—how much more summoning, arresting, examining, and flogging, robbing them of their season!
translations.idiomatic: If even travelers must pause then, how much worse to drag farmers off for torture during planting season?

sentences.zh: 比年水旱傷稼，人饑流冗。

translations.literal: In recent years flood and drought have harmed crops; people hunger and drift idle.
translations.idiomatic: Years of flood and drought have ruined harvests and driven people into famine and wandering.

sentences.zh: 今始夏，百谷權輿，陽氣胎養之時。

translations.literal: Now it is early summer; the hundred grains are beginning; it is the time when Yang qi embryos and nurtures.
translations.idiomatic: Early summer is when every crop shoots forth and Yang qi nurtures new growth.

sentences.zh: 自三月以來，陰寒不暖，物當化變而不被和氣。

translations.literal: From the third month onward, Yin cold has not warmed; creatures ought to transform and change yet do not receive harmonious qi.
translations.idiomatic: Since the third month chill has lingered—growth should be transforming yet harmonious qi never reaches it.

sentences.zh: 《月令》：『孟夏斷薄刑，出輕系。

translations.literal: The Monthly Ordinances says, "In first summer carry out light punishments and release light bonds."
translations.idiomatic: The Monthly Ordinances prescribe light punishments and freeing minor detainees in the first month of summer.

sentences.zh: 行秋令則苦雨數來，五谷不熟。

translations.literal: If autumn ordinances are practiced then bitter rains come frequently and the five grains do not ripen.
translations.idiomatic: If you impose autumn policies in summer, bitter rains will fall and the five grains will fail.

sentences.zh: 』又曰：『仲夏挺重囚，益其食。

translations.literal: " It also says, "In mid-summer extend heavy prisoners and increase their food."
translations.idiomatic: " It adds: "In mid-summer review serious prisoners and improve their rations."

sentences.zh: 行秋令則草木零落，人傷於疫。

translations.literal: If autumn ordinances are practiced then plants and trees shed leaves and people are harmed by epidemic.
translations.idiomatic: Autumn policies in summer make vegetation wither and spread plague among the people.

sentences.zh: 』夫斷薄刑者，謂其輕罪已正，不欲令久系，故時斷之也。

translations.literal: " Now cutting light punishments means their light crimes are already rectified; one does not wish to let them long remain bound; therefore one cuts them off seasonally.
translations.idiomatic: " Light sentences mean minor guilt already settled—we loosen bonds promptly rather than leave petty offenders languishing.

sentences.zh: 臣愚以為今孟夏之制，可從此令，其決獄案考，皆以立秋為斷，以順時節，育成萬物，則天地以和，刑罰以清矣。

translations.literal: Your servant foolishly thinks that today's first-summer institutions may follow this ordinance; deciding prison cases and examining investigations—take Autumn Begins as the limit—in order to comply with seasonal nodes and nurture completion of the myriad things—then Heaven and Earth will harmonize and punishments will be clear.
translations.idiomatic: I urge that while early-summer rules follow the Monthly Ordinances, actual trials and investigations should halt until Autumn Begins—honoring the seasons will let things grow and keep justice clear.

sentences.zh: 初，肅宗時，斷獄皆以冬至之前，自後論者互多駁異。

translations.literal: Formerly, in Emperor Suzong's time, cutting prison cases was all before the winter solstice; afterward debaters mutually increased refutations and differences.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Zhang capital cases had to conclude before the winter solstice; later scholars quarreled endlessly over the rule.

sentences.zh: 鄧太後詔公卿以下會議，恭議奏曰：

translations.literal: Empress Dowager Deng ordered dukes and ministers downward to assemble in conference; Gong debated and memorialized, saying:
translations.idiomatic: Empress Dowager Deng summoned the high ministers to debate the matter; Gong submitted his opinion:

sentences.zh: 夫陰陽之氣，相扶而行，發動用事，各有時節。

translations.literal: Now Yin and Yang qi move supporting one another; when they arise and act in affairs each has its seasonal node.
translations.idiomatic: Yin and Yang sustain each other—every activity has its proper season.

sentences.zh: 若不當其時，則物隨而傷。

translations.literal: If they do not match their season, then creatures follow and are harmed.
translations.idiomatic: Act out of season and creation itself suffers.

sentences.zh: 王者雖質文不同，而茲道無變，四時之政，行之若一。

translations.literal: Though kings differ in substance and pattern, this Way does not change; the governance of the four seasons—carrying it out is like one.
translations.idiomatic: Kings may vary in ritual style, but seasonal governance remains one seamless principle.

sentences.zh: 《月令》，周世所造，而所據皆夏之時也，其變者為正朔、服色、犧牲、徽號、器械而已。

translations.literal: The Monthly Ordinances were fashioned by the Zhou age, yet what they rely on are all Xia seasons; what varies are correct beginning month, garment colors, sacrificial victims, insignia titles, and instruments only.
translations.idiomatic: The Zhou Monthly Ordinances follow the Xia calendar for seasons—only the calendar start, robes, sacrifices, titles, and ritual gear changed with each dynasty.

sentences.zh: 故曰：『殷因於夏禮，周因於殷禮，所損益可知也。

translations.literal: Therefore it is said, "Yin inherited from Xia ritual; Zhou inherited from Yin ritual—the decreases and increases may be known."
translations.idiomatic: As Confucius said: "The Yin revised Xia ritual and the Zhou revised Yin—each tweak can be traced."

sentences.zh: 』《易》曰：『潛龍勿用。

translations.literal: " The Changes says, "Hidden dragon—do not employ."
translations.idiomatic: " The Book of Changes warns: "The dragon lying hidden—do not act yet."

sentences.zh: 』言十一月、十二月陽氣潛臧，未得用事。

translations.literal: " It means in the eleventh and twelfth months Yang qi lies hidden and stored; it has not yet obtained to act in affairs.
translations.idiomatic: " In the eleventh and twelfth months Yang remains submerged—too weak for decisive action.

sentences.zh: 雖煦噓萬物，養其根荄，而猶盛陰在上，地凍水冰，陽氣否隔，閉而成冬。

translations.literal: Although warming and breathing the myriad things, nurturing their roots and stems, yet still abundant Yin is above; earth freezes and water turns to ice; Yang qi is blocked and separated; closed off thereby forming winter.
translations.idiomatic: Though Yang gingerly warms the roots beneath, Yin still dominates above—earth freezes, rivers ice over, and Yang stays pent up through winter.

sentences.zh: 故曰：『履霜堅冰，陰始凝也。

translations.literal: Therefore it is said, "Treading frost—hard ice—Yin begins to congeal."
translations.idiomatic: Hence the Changes: "Step on frost—know that ice follows—Yin is gathering."

sentences.zh: 馴致其道，至堅冰也。

translations.literal: Gradually extending its Way reaches hard ice.
translations.idiomatic: Let it continue along that path and solid ice forms.

sentences.zh: 』言五月微陰始起，至十一月堅冰至也。

translations.literal: " It means in the fifth month faint Yin first arises; by the eleventh month hard ice arrives.
translations.idiomatic: " Faint Yin stirs in the fifth month and culminates in November's ice.

sentences.zh: 夫王者之作，因時為法。

translations.literal: Now the king's undertakings take seasons as law.
translations.idiomatic: The sage king legislates with the seasons.

sentences.zh: 孝章皇帝深惟古人之道，助三正之微，定律著令，冀承天心，順物性命，以致時雍。

translations.literal: Emperor Zhang deeply pondered the Way of ancients, assisting the subtlety of the three beginnings; fixing statutes and establishing orders; hoping to inherit Heaven's heart, comply with creatures' natures and lives, so as to bring seasonal harmony.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Zhang pondered antiquity, harmonized the three beginnings in law, and hoped to align Heaven with the nature of things for universal peace.

sentences.zh: 然從變改以來，年歲不熟，谷價常貴，人不寧安。

translations.literal: Yet since following changes and alterations, years and harvests have not ripened; grain prices are constantly dear; people are not tranquil and secure.
translations.idiomatic: Yet since those reforms harvests have failed, grain stays costly, and the people know no peace.

sentences.zh: 小吏不與國同心者，率入十一月得死罪賊，不問曲直，便即格殺，雖有疑罪，不復讞正。

translations.literal: Petty clerks who do not share one heart with the state—generally entering the eleventh month when obtaining capital-crime bandits—without asking crooked or straight, immediately strike and kill; although there are doubtful crimes, they no longer retry rectification.
translations.idiomatic: Petty officials who ignore the spirit of the law routinely execute supposed bandits each November without trial—even doubtful cases skip appellate review.

sentences.zh: 一夫籲嗟，王道為虧，況於眾乎？

translations.literal: One man's sighing—Kingly Way is thereby deficient—how much more the multitude?
translations.idiomatic: A single unjust execution wounds the royal Way—what then when thousands die?

sentences.zh: 《易》十一月『君子以議獄緩死』。

translations.literal: The Changes, eleventh month: "The noble man deliberates on prisons and slows death."
translations.idiomatic: The Changes for the eleventh month says the gentleman deliberates cases and stays executions.

sentences.zh: 可令疑罪使詳其法，大辟之科，盡冬月乃斷。

translations.literal: It is possible to order doubtful crimes to elaborate their laws; capital statutes—finish only by winter months before cutting off.
translations.idiomatic: Let doubtful charges receive full review and reserve executions for the depth of winter alone.

sentences.zh: 其立春在十二月中者，勿以報囚如故事。

translations.literal: Those whose Spring Begins falls within the twelfth month—do not report prisoners according to precedent.
translations.idiomatic: When Spring Begins falls in the twelfth month, suspend the usual winter execution reports.

sentences.zh: 後卒施行。

translations.literal: Later it was finally implemented.
translations.idiomatic: The court eventually adopted his proposal.

sentences.zh: 恭再在公位，選辟高第，至列卿郡守者數十人。

translations.literal: Gong twice was in public office; selections and summonses of high grade—those reaching ministers and grand administrators numbered several tens.
translations.idiomatic: Twice at court Gong nominated talent—dozens rose to ministerial or grand-administrator rank.

sentences.zh: 而其耆舊大姓，或不蒙薦舉，至有怨望者。

translations.literal: Yet among old elders and great clans, some did not receive recommendation—there were even those who harbored resentment.
translations.idiomatic: Yet eminent local families sometimes went unnominated and nursed grievances.

sentences.zh: 恭聞之，曰：『學之不講，是否憂也。

translations.literal: When Gong heard it he said, "Not discussing learning—that is my worry."
translations.idiomatic: When Gong heard the complaints he said, "When doctrine goes untaught, I am the one who ought to worry."

sentences.zh: 諸生不有鄉舉者乎？

translations.literal: Do not the students have village recommendation?
translations.idiomatic: Do students not still earn recommendation through local exams?

sentences.zh: 』終無所言。

translations.literal: " In the end he said nothing more.
translations.idiomatic: " And he said nothing further.

sentences.zh: 恭性謙退，奏議依經，潛有補益，然終不自顯，故不以剛直為稱。

translations.literal: Gong by nature was humble and yielding; memorials and deliberations relied on the classics; secretly there was benefit; yet in the end he did not display himself; therefore he was not famed as rigidly upright.
translations.idiomatic: Humble by nature, he grounded memorials in the classics and quietly shaped policy without seeking fame—so few called him blunt or harsh.

sentences.zh: 三年，以老病策罷。

translations.literal: In the third year, on account of old age and illness he was dismissed by edict.
translations.idiomatic: In the third year age and illness forced his retirement by imperial order.

sentences.zh: 六年，年八十一，卒於家。

translations.literal: In the sixth year, aged eighty-one, he died at home.
translations.idiomatic: He died at home in the sixth year at eighty-one.

sentences.zh: 以兩子為郎。

translations.literal: Two sons were made Gentlemen.
translations.idiomatic: The court appointed two of his sons as Gentlemen.

sentences.zh: 長子謙，為隴西太守，有名績。

translations.literal: The eldest son Qian was grand administrator of Longxi and had famed accomplishments.
translations.idiomatic: His eldest son Qian served as grand administrator of Longxi with a distinguished record.

sentences.zh: 謙子旭，官至太仆，從獻帝西入關，與司徒王允同謀共誅董卓。

translations.literal: Qian's son Xu, office reached Grand Coachman; following Emperor Xian west entering the passes, together with Minister of Education Wang Yun they plotted jointly to execute Dong Zhuo.
translations.idiomatic: His son Xu rose to Grand Coachman; he followed Emperor Xian west through the passes and helped Minister Wang Yun kill Dong Zhuo.

sentences.zh: 及李傕入長安，旭與允俱遇害。

translations.literal: When Li Jue entered Chang'an, Xu together with Yun alike encountered murder.
translations.idiomatic: When Li Jue seized Chang'an, Xu died alongside Wang Yun.

sentences.zh: 丕字叔陵，性沈深好學，孳孳不倦，遂杜絕交遊，不答候問之禮。

translations.literal: Pi, courtesy name Shuling, by nature was deep and fond of learning, diligent without weariness; therefore he cut off socializing and did not answer courtesy visits.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Pi, styled Shuling, was studious and grave—so immersed that he shut out society and ignored callers.

sentences.zh: 士友常以此短之，而丕欣然自得。

translations.literal: Gentry friends often faulted him for this, yet Pi was pleased and self-content.
translations.idiomatic: Scholar friends criticized his rudeness; Pi remained cheerfully indifferent.

sentences.zh: 遂兼通《五經》，以《魯詩》、《尚書》教授，為當世名儒。

translations.literal: Thereupon he comprehensively penetrated the Five Classics; teaching by means of the Lu Odes and the Documents, he became a famed Confucian of the age.
translations.idiomatic: He mastered the Five Classics and taught the Lu Odes and Documents—becoming a celebrated scholar of his day.

sentences.zh: 後歸郡，為督郵功曹，所事之將，無不師友待之。

translations.literal: Later he returned to his commandery and became merit clerk for the inspector; generals whom he served—none failed to treat him as teacher and friend.
translations.idiomatic: Back home he served as merit clerk to the inspector; every commander he advised treated him as mentor and friend.

sentences.zh: 建初元年，肅宗詔舉賢良方正，大司農劉寬舉丕。

translations.literal: In the first year of Jianchu, Emperor Suzong ordered recommendation of worthy and upright men; Minister of Agriculture Liu Kuan recommended Pi.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianchu 1 Emperor Zhang sought worthy candidates; Minister of Agriculture Liu Kuan nominated Lu Pi.

sentences.zh: 時對策者百有餘人，唯丕在高第，除為議郎，遷新野令。

translations.literal: At that time those answering policy questions were over a hundred; only Pi was in the highest grade; he was appointed Consultant and promoted to magistrate of Xinye.
translations.idiomatic: Among more than a hundred examination answers Pi alone ranked top; he became a Consultant and then magistrate of Xinye.

sentences.zh: 視事期年，州課第一，擢拜青州刺史。

translations.literal: After one full year in office, provincial evaluation ranked him first; he was elevated and appointed Inspector of Qing province.
translations.idiomatic: After one year his province ranked his performance first and elevated him to Inspector of Qing.

sentences.zh: 務在表賢明，慎刑罰。

translations.literal: His efforts lay in displaying worthiness and brightness, being cautious with punishments.
translations.idiomatic: He stressed honoring worthy men and tempering punishments.

sentences.zh: 七年，坐事下獄司寇論。

translations.literal: In the seventh year, sitting on an affair he went down to prison and the Minister of Justice adjudicated.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh year he landed in prison on a charge judged by the Minister of Justice.

sentences.zh: 永元二年，遷東郡太守。

translations.literal: In the second year of Yongyuan he was promoted to grand administrator of Dong commandery.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongyuan 2 he became grand administrator of Dong commandery.

sentences.zh: 丕在二郡，為人修通溉灌，百姓殷富。

translations.literal: While holding two successive posts Pi dredged channels and restored irrigation; the people grew wealthy.
translations.idiomatic: Across two commanderies Lu Pi dredged ditches and repaired irrigation; his people grew prosperous.

sentences.zh: 數薦達幽隱名士。

translations.literal: Repeatedly he recommended and advanced concealed eminent scholars.
translations.idiomatic: He repeatedly brought hidden talent to the court's attention.

sentences.zh: 明年，拜陳留太守。

translations.literal: The next year he was appointed grand administrator of Chenliu.
translations.idiomatic: The following year he became grand administrator of Chenliu.

sentences.zh: 視事三期，後坐稟貧人不實，征司寇論。

translations.literal: After three full terms in office, later he sat on distributing relief grain to the poor being untrue; he was summoned for the Minister of Justice's adjudication.
translations.idiomatic: After three terms he was charged with misreporting relief to the indigent and sent to the capital for trial by the Minister of Justice.

sentences.zh: 十一年復征，再遷中散大夫。

translations.literal: In the eleventh year he was again summoned and twice promoted to Counselor of the Palace.
translations.idiomatic: In year eleven he was recalled to office and promoted twice to Counselor of the Palace.

sentences.zh: 時，侍中賈逵薦丕道藝深明，宜見任用。

translations.literal: At this time Palace Attendant Jia Kui recommended Pi—his Way and arts were deeply clear—and ought to be seen and employed.
translations.idiomatic: Palace Attendant Jia Kui urged Pi's appointment, citing his profound mastery of doctrine and ritual.

sentences.zh: 和帝因朝會，召見諸儒，丕與侍中賈逵、尚書令黃香等相難數事，帝善丕說，罷朝，特賜冠幘履襪衣一襲。

translations.literal: Emperor He, owing to court assembly, summoned audience with the various Confucians; Pi with Palace Attendant Jia Kui, Prefect of Masters of Writing Huang Xiang, and others mutually challenged on several matters; the emperor praised Pi's explanations; court dismissed; specially granted cap, turban, shoes, socks, one suit of clothes.
translations.idiomatic: At a formal audience Emperor He convened the scholars; Pi debated several points with Jia Kui and Huang Xiang—the emperor favored Pi's answers and at dismissal gifted him a full set of cap, headcloth, footwear, and robes.

sentences.zh: 歪因上疏曰：『臣以愚頑，顯備大位，犬馬氣衰，猥得進見，論難於前，無所甄明，衣服之賜，誠為優過。

translations.literal: Pi therefore submitted a memorial saying, "Your servant by foolish stubbornness plainly fills a great position; dog-and-horse breath declines; meanly obtaining advancement and audience; debating difficulties before you—nothing clarified; as for the gift of clothing—it truly is generous excess.
translations.idiomatic: Pi memorialized: "I am unworthy of high office—my strength fails—yet I was allowed to debate before you without shedding real light on anything; the robes you gave exceed what I deserve."

sentences.zh: 臣聞說經者，傳先師之言，非從己出，不得相讓；

translations.literal: Your servant has heard that those who expound the classics transmit former teachers' words—they do not come from oneself—one must not yield to one another;
translations.idiomatic: Those who lecture on the classics transmit their masters' teaching—not personal opinion—so they cannot politely concede;

sentences.zh: 相讓則道不明，若規矩權衡之不可枉也。

translations.literal: if they yield to one another then the Way is not clear—like compass, square, scales, and beam that cannot be bent.
translations.idiomatic: yield on doctrine and truth blurs—like bending a level or scales.

sentences.zh: 難者必明其據，說者務立其義，浮華無用之言不陳於前，故精思不勞而道術愈章。

translations.literal: Challengers must clarify their grounds; expounders must establish their meanings; flashy useless words are not arrayed before one—therefore refined thought without strain and Way and arts become more manifest.
translations.idiomatic: Let challengers cite evidence and speakers nail down meaning—ban empty rhetoric—so truth emerges without exhaustive debate.

sentences.zh: 法異者，各令自說師法，博觀其義。

translations.literal: Where teachings differ, order each to expound his master's method; broadly observe their meanings.
translations.idiomatic: Where schools disagree, let each state its master's reading and compare meanings broadly.

sentences.zh: 覽詩人之旨意，察《雅》、《頌》之終始，明舜、禹、臯陶之相戒，顯周公、箕子之所陳，觀乎人文，化成天下。

translations.literal: Peruse the poets' aims; examine Odes and Eulogia from beginning to end; clarify Shun, Yu, and Gao Yao's mutual admonitions; display what Duke of Zhou and Jizi stated; observe human patterns therein and transform and complete the realm.
translations.idiomatic: Survey the Odes for intent, trace Hymns and Eulogia through to their lessons from Shun, Yu, Gao Yao, the Duke of Zhou, and Jizi—therein lies the pattern that civilizes the world.

sentences.zh: 陛下既廣納謇謇以開四聰，無令芻蕘以言得罪；

translations.literal: Your Majesty already broadly admits blunt remonstrance to open fourfold hearing—do not cause grass-cutters and firewood-gatherers to suffer offense for speaking;
translations.idiomatic: You welcome blunt counsel from every quarter—do not let plain folk be punished for speaking truth;

sentences.zh: 既顯巖穴以求仁賢，無使幽遠獨有遺失。』

translations.literal: already display cliff caves to seek humane worthies—do not let the hidden and distant alone suffer omission."
translations.idiomatic: you summon recluses from their caves—do not leave the obscure without a hearing."

sentences.zh: 十三年，遷為侍中，免。

translations.literal: In the thirteenth year he was promoted to Palace Attendant; dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: In year thirteen he rose to Palace Attendant, then was dismissed.

sentences.zh: 永初二年，詔公卿舉儒術篤學者，大將軍鄧騭舉丕，再遷，復為侍中、左中郎將，再為三老。

translations.literal: In the second year of Yongchu an edict ordered dukes and ministers to recommend those steeped in Confucian learning; Grand General Deng Zhi recommended Pi; after further promotions he again became Palace Attendant and Left Leader of Gentlemen and was honored once more among the Three Elders.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongchu 2 the court sought distinguished scholars; Deng Zhi nominated Pi, who rose again to Palace Attendant and Left Leader of Gentlemen and received renewed recognition as an Elder of the court.

sentences.zh: 五年，年七十五，卒於官。

translations.literal: In the fifth year, aged seventy-five, he died in office.
translations.idiomatic: He died in office in year five at seventy-five.

sentences.zh: 魏霸字喬卿，濟陰句陽人也。

translations.literal: Wei Ba, courtesy name Qiaoqing, was a man of Gouyang in Jiyin.
translations.idiomatic: Wei Ba, styled Qiaoqing, came from Gouyang in Jiyin commandery.

sentences.zh: 世有禮義。

translations.literal: His family for generations possessed ritual propriety.
translations.idiomatic: His family had long upheld ritual and moral duty.

sentences.zh: 霸少喪親，兄弟同居，州裏慕其雍和。

translations.literal: Ba young lost parents; brothers dwelt together; the province admired their harmony.
translations.idiomatic: Orphaned young, he lived with his brothers in harmony that the whole district admired.

sentences.zh: 建初中，舉孝廉，八遷，和帝時為巨鹿太守。

translations.literal: In mid Jianchu he was recommended Filial and Incorrupt; eight promotions; under Emperor He he became grand administrator of Julu.
translations.idiomatic: Recommended as Filial and Incorrupt during Jianchu, he rose through eight posts to grand administrator of Julu under Emperor He.

sentences.zh: 以簡樸寬恕為政。

translations.literal: He took simplicity and forgiveness as governance.
translations.idiomatic: He governed with plain honesty and lenience.

sentences.zh: 掾史有過，霸先誨其失，不改者乃罷之。

translations.literal: When clerks erred, Ba first instructed them in their faults; those who did not reform—only then did he dismiss them.
translations.idiomatic: When subordinates slipped, he corrected them first and dismissed only those who refused to mend their ways.

sentences.zh: 吏或相毀訴，霸輒稱它吏之長，終不及人短，言者懷慚，譖訟遂息。

translations.literal: When officials sometimes mutually slandered and sued, Ba always praised other clerks' strengths and never reached others' shortcomings; those who spoke felt shame; slander lawsuits then ceased.
translations.idiomatic: When officials denounced one another, Ba praised each man's strengths and ignored faults until the accusers grew ashamed and dropped their suits.

sentences.zh: 永元十六年，征拜將作大匠。

translations.literal: In the sixteenth year of Yongyuan he was summoned and appointed Superintendent of Construction.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongyuan 16 he was summoned to serve as Superintendent of Imperial Works.

sentences.zh: 明年，和帝崩，典作順陵。

translations.literal: The next year Emperor He died; he supervised construction of the Shun Mausoleum.
translations.idiomatic: The following year Emperor He died; Wei supervised work on Empress Dowager Dou's Shun tomb.

sentences.zh: 時盛冬地凍，中使督促，數罰縣吏以厲霸。

translations.literal: At that time deep winter froze the earth; palace envoys urged supervision; repeatedly they punished county clerks to sharpen Ba.
translations.idiomatic: Deep winter froze the ground; eunuch overseers drove the work and punished county clerks repeatedly to pressure Wei.

sentences.zh: 霸撫循而已，初不切責，而反勞之曰：『令諸卿被辱，大匠過也。

translations.literal: Ba comforted and followed them only; at first he did not sharply blame; instead he consoled them, saying, "Having caused you gentlemen to suffer insult—is the Superintendent's fault.
translations.idiomatic: Wei soothed his men instead of rebuking them: "If you were humiliated, the fault is mine as superintendent."

sentences.zh: 』吏皆懷恩，力作倍功。

translations.literal: " The clerks all cherished grace; exerted labor twofold in accomplishment.
translations.idiomatic: " Grateful, the crews doubled their efforts.

sentences.zh: 劉寬字文饒，弘農華陰人也。

translations.literal: Liu Kuan, courtesy name Wenrao, was a man of Huayin in Hongnong.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Kuan, styled Wenrao, was a native of Huayin in Hongnong.

sentences.zh: 父崎，順帝時為司徒。

translations.literal: His father Qi, in Emperor Shun's time, was Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: His father Liu Qi served as Minister of Education under Emperor Shun.

sentences.zh: 寬嘗行，有人失牛者，乃就寬車中認之。

translations.literal: Kuan once was traveling; someone who had lost an ox then entered Kuan's carriage and claimed it.
translations.idiomatic: Once when Liu Kuan was out riding, a man who had lost an ox insisted the animal was tied to Kuan's cart.

sentences.zh: 寬無所言，下駕步歸。

translations.literal: Kuan said nothing; he dismounted his carriage and walked home.
translations.idiomatic: Kuan said nothing—he got down from his carriage and walked home on foot.

sentences.zh: 有頃，認者得牛而送還，叩頭謝曰：『慚負長者，隨所刑罪。

translations.literal: After a moment the claimant found his ox and returned it, kowtowing in apology and saying, "Ashamed to have wronged a man of mature virtue—I submit to whatever punishment."
translations.idiomatic: Soon the man found his own ox and brought Kuan's mount back, kowtowing: "I have wronged a gentleman—punish me as you see fit."

sentences.zh: 』寬曰：『物有相類，事容脫誤，幸勞見歸，何為謝之？

translations.literal: " Kuan said, "Things have likenesses; affairs allow slip and error; fortunately you labored to return it—why apologize?
translations.idiomatic: " Kuan replied, "Animals often resemble one another—mistakes happen. Thank you for bringing him back—there is nothing to apologize for."

sentences.zh: 』州裏服其不校。

translations.literal: " The province admired his not retaliating.
translations.idiomatic: " The district marveled at his refusal to take offense.

sentences.zh: 桓帝時，大將軍辟，五遷司徒長史。

translations.literal: In Emperor Huan's time the Grand General summoned him; after five promotions he became chief clerk to the Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Huan the Grand General recruited him; after five promotions he became chief clerk to the Minister of Education.

sentences.zh: 時、京師地震，特見詢問。

translations.literal: At that time the capital suffered earthquake; he was specially seen and questioned.
translations.idiomatic: When an earthquake struck the capital he was summoned for special consultation.

sentences.zh: 再遷，出為東海相。

translations.literal: Again promoted; went out as chancellor of Donghai.
translations.idiomatic: Further promoted, he left the capital as chancellor of the Donghai kingdom.

sentences.zh: 延熹八年，征拜尚書令，遷南陽太守。

translations.literal: In the eighth year of Yanxi he was summoned and appointed Prefect of Masters of Writing; promoted to grand administrator of Nanyang.
translations.idiomatic: In Yanxi 8 he was recalled as Prefect of Masters of Writing, then transferred to grand administrator of Nanyang.

sentences.zh: 典歷三郡，溫仁多恕，雖在倉卒，未嘗疾言遽色。

translations.literal: Presiding over three commanderies in succession, warm and humane with much forgiveness—although in sudden haste he never had harsh words or abrupt expression.
translations.idiomatic: Across three commanderies he was gentle and forgiving—even in crisis he never snapped or scowled.

sentences.zh: 常以為『齊之以刑，民免而無恥。

translations.literal: He constantly took as principle "If you regulate them with punishments, the people avoid them but have no sense of shame."
translations.idiomatic: He often quoted Confucius: "Lead only with punishments and people stay out of trouble but feel no shame."

sentences.zh: 』吏人有過，但用蒲鞭罰之，示辱而已，終不加苦。

translations.literal: " When clerks and people had faults, he only used a rattan whip to punish them—displaying shame only—in the end did not add suffering.
translations.idiomatic: " For minor faults he swatted offenders with a soft rattan whip—enough to shame them, never to injure.

sentences.zh: 事有功善，推之自下。

translations.literal: When affairs had merit and goodness, he pushed them from below.
translations.idiomatic: When something went well he credited his subordinates.

sentences.zh: 災異或見，引躬克責。

translations.literal: When disasters or prodigies sometimes appeared, he drew himself in and overcame with self-blame.
translations.idiomatic: When omens appeared he blamed himself first.

sentences.zh: 每行縣止息亭傳，輒引學官祭酒及處士諸生執經對講。

translations.literal: Whenever traveling counties he stopped to rest at courier stations—always summoned education officials, libationers, and recluses and students holding classics to face one another in lecture.
translations.idiomatic: Touring his counties he halted at post stations and convened local scholars and students for classical discussion.

sentences.zh: 見父老慰以農裏之言，少年勉以孝悌之訓。

translations.literal: Seeing elders he consoled them with words of farming hamlets; to youths he exhorted with teachings of filiality and fraternal duty.
translations.idiomatic: He urged elders with talk of farming and instructed youths in filial piety and brotherly duty.

sentences.zh: 人感德興行，日有所化。

translations.literal: People felt virtue and arose in conduct; daily there was transformation.
translations.idiomatic: Moved by his virtue, people improved day by day.

sentences.zh: 靈帝初，征拜太中大夫，傳講華光殿。

translations.literal: At the beginning of Emperor Ling he was summoned and appointed Grand Counselor of the Palace; lectured by transmission at Huaguang Hall.
translations.idiomatic: Early in Emperor Ling's reign he became Grand Counselor of the Palace and lectured on the classics at Huaguang Hall.

sentences.zh: 遷侍中，賜衣一襲。

translations.literal: Promoted to Palace Attendant; granted one suit of clothes.
translations.idiomatic: Promoted to Palace Attendant and gifted a full set of robes.

sentences.zh: 轉屯騎校尉，遷宗正，轉光祿勛。

translations.literal: Transferred to Colonel of Garrison Cavalry; promoted to Chamberlain for the Imperial Clan; transferred to Chamberlain for the Palace Revenues.
